remarks_html:
Identify yourself with BotNet.

This should be the first message sent to the server and identifies the client's bot name and password.

For server revisions 2 and newer, the server responds with S>C 0x0A BOTNET_REVISION before the response to this packet in S>C 0x01 BOTNET_LOGON.

For server revisions 4 and newer, the server closes the connection if the client is rejected instead of sending any data. Older revisions would respond with S>C 0x01 BOTNET_LOGON with Result set to 0x00 (failure).

In order to get a name and password, contact the BotNet server admin.

The Bot name may also be called the "bot ID" or "hub ID".
